provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Immigration (Visa) Regulations, 2016.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— “ Act ” means the Immigration Act ; [Cap. 54] “ visa ” has the meaning ascribed to it under section 3 of the Act ; “ Commissioner General ” has the meaning attributed to it under the Immigration Act ; “ Issuing Officer ” means any person authorised in terms of these Regulations to issue visa , or Endorsement thereof; “ Minister ” means the Minister responsible for immigration; “ Passport ” has the meaning ascribed to it under section 3 of the Act ; “ visitor ” means any foreigner who wishes to enter the United Republic for purposes prescribed under regulation 3(1) of the Immigration Regulations, 1997. Part II – Types of issuing stations and Visa controls

provisions.title: Types of Visa
provisions.content: (1) There shall be the following types of Visa— (a) Ordinary Visa; (b) Business Visa; (c) Transit Visa; and (d) Diplomatic Visa; [paragraph (d) substituted by section 3(a) of Government Notice 541 of 2020 ] (e) Student Visa. [paragraph (e) added by section 3(b) of Government Notice 541 of 2020 ] (2) The power to issue Visa shall be vested in the Commissioner General or any other person authorized as such by the Commissioner General .

provisions.title: Ordinary Visa
provisions.content: An Ordinary Visa may be issued for single entry or multiple entries to a person whose nationality requires visa to enter the United Republic as a visitor .

provisions.title: Business Visa
provisions.content: A Business Visa may be issued for single entry or multiple entries to a person whose nationality requires visa to enter the United Republic for the purpose of temporarily conducting business, trade, and profession. [section 5 amended by section 4 of Government Notice 541 of 2020 ]

provisions.title: Transit Visa
provisions.content: Transit Visa may be issued for a period not exceeding seven days to a person whose nationality requires visa to enter the United Republic for the purpose of transiting to another destination outside the United Republic.

provisions.title: Diplomatic Visa
provisions.content: Diplomatic Visa may be issued for single entry to ambassadors, embassy staff, holder of service or official passport who is on official duty and dependants to the above persons. [section 7 substituted by section 5 of Government Notice 541 of 2020 ]

provisions.title: Issuing station
provisions.content: (1) A visa may be issued in any of the following stations: (a) designated entry points as prescribed under the Third Schedule of the Immigration Regulations, 1997; (b) all Tanzania Missions abroad. (2) Without prejudice to the provisions of this regulation, visa may be issued at Tanzania Immigration Department Headquarters, Dar es Salaam, Head Office Zanzibar or any Immigration office as may be directed by the Commissioner General .

provisions.title: Student visa
provisions.content: Student Visa may be issued to a person whose nationality requires visa to enter the United Republic for purpose of undertaking training for a period not exceeding ninety days. [section 8A inserted by section 6 of Government Notice 541 of 2020 ]

provisions.title: Visa controls
provisions.content: (1) The issuance of visa for the purpose of entry into the United Republic shall be treated on the basis of the following controls— (a) a person whose country is not listed in the Third Schedule to these Regulations, shall require visa without approval to enter into the United Republic; (b) a person whose country is listed in the Third Schedule to these Regulation, shall not require visa to enter into the United republic; (c) the visa application of a person whose country is listed in the Fourth Schedule to these Regulations, shall be subject to the approval of the Commissioner General or such other person as may be directed by him. (2) Notwithstanding the provisions of subregulations (1) , a child with dual Citizenship in accordance with the Tanzania Citizenship Act shall not require visa to enter the United Republic. [Cap. 357] Part III – Application procedures, forms and fees

provisions.title: Procedures for applying Visa
provisions.content: (1) Any person who intends to travel into the United Republic and is from a country which requires visa to enter into the United Republic shall apply for visa in Form TVF.01 set out in the First Schedule. [subsection (1) amended by section 7 of Government Notice 541 of 2020 ] (2) Any person who knowingly, negligently or recklessly gives or attempts to give any information which he believes to be false, misleading or conceals any material facts in order to be issued or cause the visa to be issued to a person, commits an offence and is liable, on conviction, to a fine of not less than one million shillings or to imprisonment for a term of not less than two years or to both.

provisions.title: Fees
provisions.content: (1) There shall be paid in respect of an application for issuance of visa , a non refundable fee as prescribed in the Second Schedule to these Regulations. (2) The fees specified under the Second Schedule shall be paid before the issuance of visa . (3) The Minister may from time to time vary the fees provided for under the Second Schedule.

provisions.title: Visa format
provisions.content: A visa issued in terms of regulations 8 shall be in a format prescribed under the First Schedule to these Regulations. Part V – Conditions and validity of Visa [Please note: Numbering as in original.]

provisions.title: Visa conditions
provisions.content: A visa may be issued subject to such terms and conditions as may be specified in the respective visa .

provisions.title: Validity and expiration of Visa
provisions.content: (1) A visa shall be issued for a period not exceeding ninety days. (2) A visa may expire on the following grounds: (a) where it is issued in the Tanzania Mission and the granted period lapses prior to the date of travel by the holder; (b) where the holder is determined to be a prohibited immigrant under the Act ; (c) where the holder has been issued with a permit or pass. Part VI – Revocation of Visa

provisions.title: Revocation of Visa
provisions.content: (1) The Commissioner General may at any time revoke any visa issued under these Regulations and the holder shall not be allowed to enter, continue staying remain in the United Republic. (2) A Visa shall be revoked on the following grounds: (a) if holder is determined to be a prohibited immigrant; (b) if it is proved that the visa was obtained fraudulently or by deceit; (c) if it is determined on public interest that such visa be revoked. (3) Upon revocation, the Commissioner General shall cause the holder to be informed in writing, the decision of such revocation of the visa .

provisions.title: Visa extension
provisions.content: A visa may be extended on specific grounds for a further period of more than ninety days upon approval by the Commissioner General. [section 15A inserted by section 8 of Government Notice 541 of 2020 ]

provisions.title: General penalty
provisions.content: Any person who commits an offence under these Regulations where no other penalty is expressly provided in these Regulations shall be liable on conviction to a fine not less than two hundreds thousands Shillings or to imprisonment for a term not less than one year or to both. First Schedule (Made under regulation 10(1)) Application form Second Schedule (Made under regulation 11) Fees [Second Schedule substituted by section 9 of Government Notice 541 of 2020 ] Part I – Normal Visa code Purpose of visit Descriptions Requirments Fee in USD A: Ordinary Visa A1 Tourism This carter for all forms of tourism including: (a) Photographic tourism (b) Hunting tourism; (c) Diving tourism; (d) Conferencing Tourism; (e) Others (i) Biographic data page of an applicant’s passport; (ii) Invitation letter if any; and (iii) Return ticket 50.00 A2 Leisure & holiday, Visitors in this category commonly travel during their vacation in a year (during a special season) (i) Biographic data page Passport data of an applicant's; (ii) Inviter’s copy of passport or residence permit if any; and (iii) Return ticket 50.00 A3 Visiting friends, relatives Visitors in this group commonly travel to visit their friends and relatives who are resident or citizen of United Republic of Tanzania (i) Biographic data page data of an applicant’s Passport; (ii) An invitation letter from a host (iii) A host’s copy of passport/National ID or Residence permit; and (iv) Return ticket. 50.00 A4 Meetings & Workshops; Individuals who are: (a) Attendant; (b) Participants; (c) Facilitators; (d) Trainers; (e) Organizers; (f) Logistics issues; (g) Others. (i) Biographic data page of an applicant’s passport; (ii) Invitation letter ; and (iii) Return ticket 50.00 A5 Exhibitions and Trade Fairs Organized by Government & Private Institutions Individuals participating in Exhibitions as: (a) Participants; (b) Facilitators; (c) Attendants; (d) Business transactions; (i) Biographic data page of an applicant’s passport; (ii) Invitation letter; and (iii) Return ticket. 50.00 A6 Humanitarians and charity activities Persons volunteering in a recognized charity organization for a period not exceeding three months; (i) Biographic data page data of an applicant's Passport; (ii) invitation letter from a registered host institution; and (iii) Return ticket. 50.00 A7 Health treatment Visitors who come to undergo medical treatment or check up in Tanzania. (i) Biographic data page data of an applicant's Passport; (ii) Valid copy of residence; permit-or Passport copy/ID of a host citizen (if any); (iii) A covering letter/referral letter from a recognized hospital/medical institution if any; (iv) A copy of previous medical report (If any); and (v) Return ticket 50.00 A8 Family Visit This include members of family such as; (a) Parents; (b) Father; (c) Mother; (d) Children; (e) Friend; (f) Spouse; (g) An adult coming to be cared by relatives; (h) Others (In relation to family i.e. extended family); (i) Biographic data page data of an applicant’s Passport (ii) A copy of Passport/National ID of Tanzanian family member or (iii) A copy of birth certificates for children; (iv) A copy of marriage certificate for spouses; and (v) Return ticket. 50.00 B: Business Visa Code Purpose Description Requirments Fee B1 Technical activities with registered private Companies/Institutions All technical assignments: (a) Servicing machines; (b) Machine installation; (c) Machine erecting; (d) Fixing of machine; (e) Others (relating to technical issues not mentioned) (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) An invitation letter from a registered company; (iii) Return ticket; and (iv) Contract for service (if any). 250.00 B2 Business Consultants (a) Advisors in all sectors of the economy; (b) Policy developers; (c) Developing a business plan or proposals; (d) Quality Assurance; (e) Company Secretary; (f) Draft men; (g) Others (Including all not mentioned) (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) An invitation letter from a registered institution; and (iii) Return ticket. 250.00 B3 Academic purposes (a) External Supervisors; (b) External Examiners; (c) Advisors (d) Feasibility study; (e) Consultants; and (f) Others. (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) An invitation letter; and (iii) Return ticket. 250.00 B4 Facilitate trade in services Individuals providing services using their professions i.e.: (a) Auditing; (b) Engineering; (c) Accounts (d) Agriculture (e) Doctors (f) Others (Include those not mentioned) (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) An invitation letter from a registered Institution; (iii) Contract for service; (iv) Return ticket. 250.00 B5 Setting up a business Employing staff for the first time: (a) Looking for business premises; and (b) Register a company. (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) Applicant’s letter detailing the nature of business he/she wishes to undertake; and (iii) Return ticket. 250.00 B6 Implementation of technical & Service contract Implementation of contract for services between vendors and their clients (i) Biographic data page data of an applicant's Passport; (ii) A copy of contract for service; (iii) Invitation letter from a client Institution; and (iv) Return ticket. 250.00 B7 Business Research (a) Research for various business and investment opportunities; (b) Feasibility Study. (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) Invitation letter from a registered host Institution (iii) permission from a competent authority if any; and (iv) Return ticket. 250.00 B8 Pre-skills test. This covers prospective foreign employees undergoing recruitment process before signing contract of employment with employers (i) Biographic data page of an applicant’s passport; (ii) Invitation letter; (iii) Contract for service; and (iv) Return ticket. 250.00 B9 Managing a Ltd company Provisional management of a company: (a) Business consultation; (b) Closing a company; (c) Business negotiation; and (d) Tax return issues. (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport (ii) Invitation Letter from a Host Institution/Client; and (iii) Return ticket 250.00 C: Student Visa Code Purpose Description Requirments Fee C1 Intern student Less than 90 days single (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) Covering letter from a legally registered Institution/Company; and (iii) Return ticket 50.00 C2 Volunteer Student Three months-single (i) Biographic data page of an applicant’s passport; (ii) A letter of introduction from a relevant host Institution; and (iii) Return ticket. 50.00 C3 Student enrolled in public institutions Three months-single (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) Admission letter; (iii) Evidence of Sponsorship/Scholarship; (iv) Parent consent for minor; (v) Copy of valid Residence permit of parent or guardian; and (vi) Return ticket. 50.00 C4 Students admitted in an established and Private Training institutions Three months-single (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport (ii) Admission letter; (iii) Evidence of Sponsorship/Scholarship; (iv) Parent's consent for minor; (v) Copy of valid Residence permit of parent or guardian; and (vi) Return ticket. 50.00 C5 Exchange Students Subject to study period (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (iii) A letter of introduction from a sending and receiving Institution; (iv) MOU or an agreement for the Exchange Program; and (v) Open return ticket. Gratis C6 Intern Student Three to twenty four months-multiple (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) Admission letter; and (iii) Open return ticket. 250.00 C7 Student enrolled in Public Institutions Three months to twenty four months multiple (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) Admission letter; (iii) Evidence of Sponsorship/Scholarship; (iv) Parent's consent for minor; (v) Copy of valid Residence permit of parent or guardian; and (vi) Open return ticket. 250.00 C8 Volunteer Student Three months to twenty four months multiple (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) Invitation letter from a legally registered Institution/Company; and (iii) Open return ticket. 250.00 C9 Students admitted in an established and private training institutions Three months to twenty four month smultiple (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) Admission letter; (iii) Evidence of Sponsorship/Scholarship; (iv) Parent's consent for minor; (v) Copy of valid Residence permit of parent or guardian; and (vi) Open return ticket. 250.00 C10 Research Student Six months-multiple (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) Covering letter from a legally registered Institution/Company; (iii) A letter of Introduction from the relevant university/College/Institute; and (iv) Open return ticket. 300.00 C11 Research student (PhD students and others from higher learning institutions and private individuals), Three to twenty four months-multiple (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; (ii) Invitation letter from a legally registered Institution/ Company or University/college ; (iii) Research Permit from COSTECH; and (iv) Open return ticket. 550.00 D: Diplomatic Visa Code Purpose Requirments Fee D1 Ambassadors (a) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport (b) A letter of approval from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. GRATIS D2 Embassy Staff (a) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport (b) A letter of approval from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. GRATIS D3 Dependants of D1 and D2 (a) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport; and (b) Notaries certificate. GRATIS D4 International Organizations Staffs (a) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport (b) A letter of approval from the relevant Organization GRATIS D5 Dependants of D4 (a) Biographic data page of an applicant’s passport (b) Notaries certificate. GRATIS D6 Holder of Diplomatic, Service or Official Passport whose URT has Visa abolition agreement (a) Biographic data page of an applicant's passport GRATIS E: Multiple entry Visa Code Purpose Description Requirments Fees in USD E1 Directors of company registered in Tanzania This carter for persons including: (a) Nonresidents investors & Directors; (b) Authorized agents of nonresidents investors; (c) Representat ives of Multinational companies; and (d) Others; (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's Passport; (ii) A copy of registration certificate/certificate of compliance; (iii) Proof of being a Director, an agent, representative, shareholders, (iv) Invitation letter if any or; 100.00 E2 Regular family visits (a) Spouse; (b) Children of foreigners residing in Tanzania; (c) Dependents of Tanzania citizens (Nonresidents aliens); nd (d) Family relatives; and (e) Others (i) Biographic data page data of an applicant's Passport; (ii) A copy of marriage certificate (Spouse); (iii) A copy of birth certificate (Child); (iv) An invitation letter from a host; and (v) A host's copy of passport/National ID or Residence permit. 100.00 E3 Bilateral Agreements This carters for the implementation of the existing economic and social bilateral agreements (i) Biographic data page of an applicant’s Passport; (ii) A copy of registration certificate/certificate of compliance; and (iii) Invitation letter if any. 100.00 E4 Consultancy with registered companies, NGO’s and other Institutions This carters for the provision of social and economical consultancy services (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's Passport; (ii) A copy of registration certificate/certificate of compliance; (iii) Invitation letter if any or; 100.00 E5 Consultancy with Government Ministries and Departments This carter for the persons dealing with:— (a) Policy development & reforms; (b) Advisory services; (c) Technical issues; or (d) Others (i) Biographic data page of an applicant's Passport; and (ii) Invitation letter
